Most Presentable and One of Only Two Known
17080
DENMARK. 2 Speciedaler, 1668. Copenhagen Mint. Frederik III. NGC AU-55. Dav-3558; KM-302 (wrong illustra-
tion); Hede-79A; Sieg-95.1; Schou-9; Aagaard-111.1; Bruun-6643. Weight: 56.76 gms. Mintmaster: Frederik Caspar 
Herbach (FCH). Engraver: Jeremias Hercules. A most presentable piece of this commanding and majestic type with the 
king clad in armor with a lion’s head on his shoulder. It was only minted for two years, 1668 and 1669, and only about a 
handful examples in total seem to have survived. Of the present date only one other piece has been recorded by Aagaard, 
the one at the Danish National Museum. The date is rather unusually tucked away at the horizontal arms of the cross.
€12,000-€16,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).

========== PAGE 59 ==========

 Bid Online at www.StacksBowers.com P age 43
Session I • Tuesday , March 24, 2026 • 12:00  PM ET / 5:00  PM CET
Finest Norwegian Ducat of All in Private Ownership
A Masterpiece of Scandinavian Baroque Portraiture
17085
NORW AY . 2 Ducat, ND (1669-1670?). Christiania Mint. Frederik III. NGC MS-66H. Fr-5b (Norway); Fr-96 (Den-
mark); KM-A119 (Norway, mistakenly listed with date 1670); Hede-39 (Denmark); Sieg-139 (Denmark); Sieg-73 (Nor-
way); NMD-5A; Schou-6; Aagaard-7.5; Bruun-6738. Weight: 6.55 gms. Mintmaster: Frederik Grüner. Engraver: Jonas 
Lunder. An almost unfathomable Gem 66-star with a stupendous luster bouncing from the perfect fields. A warm or-
ange toning delicately hugs the untouched and lightly mat devices further adding to the unparalleled eye appeal. To the 
best of our knowledge the present piece is nothing less than the finest Norwegian Ducat of all in private ownership.
An excessively rare type of which Aagaard only knows of four examples struck with this beautiful obverse die: One in 
the Danish National Museum, one in the Hermitage, the present, and one other piece which Bruun acquired with the 
Guildal collection. This was sold as #2760 in the Bruun collection of duplicates and have since then passed through 
important collections like Ragoczy and Zinck.
As this type doesn’t bear St. Olavs’ axe-bearing lion or for that matter other design elements normally associated with Nor-
way it has until recently been assumed to have been struck in Copenhagen. Jonas Lunder, who worked as an engraver at 
the Mint in Christiania from c. 1664 - 1682, was the first of a Norwegian “dynasty” of engravers comprising Anders Jonsen 
Lunder, the Elder, Gabriel Andersen Lunder, and Anders Jonsen Lunder, the Younger.
€150,000-€200,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).

========== PAGE 60 ==========

Page 44 Stack’s Bowers Galleries
The L.  E. Bruun Co llection • A Corpus of Scandinavian Monetary History Part IV
Outstanding Exemplar of Immense Eye Appeal
Produced for the Danish East India Company
17086
DENMARK. Speciedaler, 1671. Copenhagen Mint. Christian V . NGC MS-62+. Dav-409; KM-317; Hede-63A; Sieg-
60.1; Schou-6; Salvesen-21; Bruun-6758. Weight: 29.06 gms. Mintmaster: Gotfred Krüger (GK). Engraver: Jeremias 
Hercules. An outstanding exemplar of immense eye-appeal. With elegant toning revealing speckles of turquoise, purple 
and gold and a splendid luster practically leaping off the surfaces, this outstanding exemplar possesses immense eye-
appeal. 
The Danish East India Company (Dansk Ostindisk Compagni) was created on the initiative of Christian IV as the first 
Danish shareholding company. The king was the largest shareholder, but among the investors were nobility and citizens in 
Denmark, Norway and the duchies. The intention with the establishment of the company was to gain a share in the trade in 
spices, silk and cotton on a par with Holland and England, and the company was given a monopoly on trade with Asia by 
sea. In 1618 the first expedition was sent, and as a result of this expedition the trading station of Tranquebar was established 
on the Coromandel coast in south-east India. In the first years the financial yield was, however, modest and the company 
was dissolved in 1650. It was not until 1668 that a ship was again sent from Denmark-Norway to Asia, and in 1670 a new 
East India Company was established. As the trading goods were predominantly paid for in silver, the newly started company 
funded the present mintage, which they had obtained the privilege to mint at the Royal Mint in Copenhagen. At the end of 
the 17th century, trade experienced better business conditions and it was possible to send out an average of two ships per 
year.
€18,000-€22,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Vilhelm Bergsøe Collection (Copenhagen 1890) Lot # 291.

========== PAGE 61 ==========

 Bid Online at www.StacksBowers.com P age 45
Session I • Tuesday , March 24, 2026 • 12:00  PM ET / 5:00  PM CET
    
17087
DENMARK. 4 Mark (Krone), 1671. Glückstadt Mint. Christian V . NGC MS-64. Dav-3678; KM-63; H-121; Sieg-137.1; Schou-23; 
Aagaard-5.1; Bruun-6772. Weight: 22.08 gms. Mintmaster: Johan Woltereck. An absolutely superior exemplar of the first Krone struck 
after the coinage reform of March 22nd 1671 when it was decided to reactivate the Glückstadt Mint after a break of 11 years. 
€700-€900
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Commissioned by Christian IV , the 1618 expedition to 
Ceylon was the first operational voyage of the Danish East 
India Company, established to secure Danish trade in Asia.

========== PAGE 62 ==========

Page 46 Stack’s Bowers Galleries
The L.  E. Bruun Co llection • A Corpus of Scandinavian Monetary History Part IV
Unrivalled Dazzling Gem Speciedaler
Extremely Rare Colonial Type
17088
DENMARK. Speciedaler, 1672. Copenhagen Mint. Christian V . NGC MS-65. Dav-410; KM-362; Hede-63C; Sieg-
60.3; Schou-7; Salvesen-22; Bruun-6778. Weight: 28.71 gms. Mintmaster: Gotfred Krüger (GK). Engraver: Jeremias 
Hercules. Words seem not to be enough to describe this dazzling Gem unrivalled among the whole series. This ever-
popular Colonial type is extremely rare with Salvesen listing only 10 examples, and to find another in such unbelievable 
condition seems unthinkable.
The Danish East India Company (Dansk Ostindisk Compagni) was created on the initiative of Christian IV as the first 
Danish shareholding company. The king was the largest shareholder, but among the investors were nobility and citizens in 
Denmark, Norway and the duchies. The intention with the establishment of the company was to gain a share in the trade in 
spices, silk and cotton on a par with Holland and England, and the company was given a monopoly on trade with Asia by 
sea. In 1618 the first expedition was sent, and as a result of this expedition the trading station of Tranquebar was established 
on the Coromandel coast in south-east India. In the first years the financial yield was, however, modest and the company 
was dissolved in 1650. It was not until 1668 that a ship was again sent from Denmark-Norway to Asia, and in 1670 a new 
East India Company was established. As the trading goods were predominantly paid for in silver, the newly started company 
funded the present mintage, which they had obtained the privilege to mint at the Royal Mint in Copenhagen. At the end of 
the 17th century, trade experienced better business conditions and it was possible to send out an average of two ships per 
year.
€28,000-€38,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Vilhelm Bergsøe Collection (Copenhagen 1890) Lot # 294.

========== PAGE 63 ==========

 Bid Online at www.StacksBowers.com P age 47
Session I • Tuesday , March 24, 2026 • 12:00  PM ET / 5:00  PM CET
    
17089
DENMARK. 1/2 Ducat, 1675. Copenhagen Mint. Christian V . 
NGC MS-63H. Fr-138; KM-354; Hede-5; Sieg-71; Schou-1; Bru-
un-6828. Weight: 1.73 gms. Mintmaster: Gotfred Krüger (GK). 
Flabbergastingly beautiful piece with full luster bouncing from 
the surfaces around the matte portrait. Extremely rare one-year 
type which was only struck as a Half Ducat.
The mintmaster Gotfred Krüger’s initials (GK) flanking the elegant 
double monogram are quite unusually rendered in a script typeface 
instead of the usual majuscules. 
€8,000-€10,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Otto Diemar Collection (Copenhagen - 1901) Lot # 1023.
Ex : V . H. Krag Collection (Copenhagen - 1887) # 16.
    
17090
DENMARK. Ducat, 1676. Glückstadt Mint. Christian V . NGC 
MS-63. Fr-134; KM-64.1; Hede-115B; Sieg-142.2; Schou-1; 
Aagaard-2.8; Bruun-6856. Weight: 3.48 gms. Mintmaster: Johan 
Woltereck (IW). A vibrantly lustrous and sharply struck Choice 
Mint State piece of a rare date.
The Ducats struck in Glückstadt differ from the ones struck in Co-
penhagen in being minted for regular trade use rather than just as 
occasion issues. 
€6,000-€8,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Frederik Chr. Bech Collection (Copenhagen - 1906) Lot # 689.
Stunningly Lustrous and Extremely Rare Ducat
17091
DENMARK. Ducat, 1683. Copenhagen Mint. Christian V . NGC MS-63. Fr-147; KM-371; Hede-10; Sieg-88; Schou-2; 
Bruun-6986. Weight: 3.45 gms. Mintmaster: Gregorius Sesemann (GS). A stunningly lustrous Choice Ducat with sub-
lime details. Excessively rare with only a very few in private hands.
The obverse die was originally cut for the 1681 issue and was also used for the 1682 Frederiksborg type (Fr-148).
€12,000-€16,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

========== PAGE 64 ==========

Page 48 Stack’s Bowers Galleries
The L.  E. Bruun Co llection • A Corpus of Scandinavian Monetary History Part IV
    
17092
DENMARK. 2 Skilling, 1686. Copenhagen Mint. Christian V . NGC MS-66. KM-383.1; Hede-54; Sieg-9.2; Schou-25; Bruun-7017. 
Mintmaster: Gregorius Seseman (GS). Engraver: Jeremias Hercules. A completely irresistible example of this one-year monogram type 
in a charming variant with flowers on both sides of the denomination. 
€800-€1,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Wonderfully Detailed Near Gem Double Ducat
Rare Colonial Interest Type
17093
DENMARK. 2 Ducat, 1688. Copenhagen Mint. Christian V . NGC MS-64. Fr-167; KM-395.2; Hede-23B; Sieg-93; 
Schou-4; Bruun-7036. Weight: 6.95 gms. Mintmaster: Gregorius Sesemann. A wonderfully detailed strike perfectly 
centered on a broad flan retaining almost full luster makes this near Gem a must have for the historically interested 
connaisseur of 17th century coins of colonial interest. Small die crack on the lower reverse as always.
The reverse offers a bird’s eye view of the Danish outpost on Africa’s Gold Coast, Fort Christiansborg (now Osu Castle), 
located in what is now Ghana along the Gulf of Guinea. The fortress became the Danish administrative headquarters of 
Guinea in 1685 and was predominantly used as a place of embarkation of enslaved local captives for the Danish colonies 
in the Caribbean.
The portrait of the king wearing a helmet is quite unique in Danish numismatics but is in fact very similar to the eques-
trian statue by the French sculptor Abraham-César Lamoreux. This statue, which can still be seen at the square of Kongens 
Nytorv in the center of Copenhagen. was erected in the same year as the present issue and could very well have served as 
inspiration.
€22,000-€26,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).

========== PAGE 65 ==========

 Bid Online at www.StacksBowers.com P age 49
Session I • Tuesday , March 24, 2026 • 12:00  PM ET / 5:00  PM CET
    
17094
DENMARK. 4 Mark (Krone), 1690. Copenhagen Mint. Christian V . NGC MS-64. Dav-3643; KM-401; Hede-90B; Sieg-46.2; 
Schou-10; Bruun-7055. Weight: 22.02 gms. Mintmaster: Christian Wineke, the Elder (CW). A gorgeous, near-Gem Krone with a spec-
tacular sheen and a highly attractive toning.
One of the earliest issues minted by Christian Wineke who didn’t receive his appointment until November 28th 1690. 
€700-€900
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Very Rare Double Ducat in Phenomenal Condition
17095
DENMARK. 2 Ducat, 1691. Copenhagen Mint. Christian V . NGC MS-64. Fr-154; KM-416.1; Hede-27A; Sieg-99; 
Schou-4; Bruun-7059. Weight: 6.94 gms. Mintmaster: Christian Wineke, the Elder (CW). A phenomenal piece nearly 
Gem and with all surfaces alight with the glow of gold, and satiny finish to the devices. By far the best of only a very few 
in private ownership.
Christian Wineke had only taken over the position as mintmaster in November 1690 and the portrait on this type by an 
unknown engraver can be seen as a forerunner to Wineke’s collaboration with Anton Meybusch that began in the following 
year.
€20,000-€25,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).

From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Joh. G. Guildal Collection (Private Sale - 1918); later published by the Danish Numismatic Society as item 1952 in his collection.
Perfectly Struck and of the Utmost Rarity
17097
DENMARK. 2 Ducat, 1693. Copenhagen Mint. Christian V . NGC AU-58. Fr-unlisted (cf. 160); KM-439; Hede-38; 
Sieg-125; Schou-2; Kold-131a; Bruun-7115. Weight: 6.90 gms. Mintmaster: Christian Wineke, the Elder. Engraver: An-
ton Meybusch. A perfectly struck large diameter Double Ducat with a luster and an eye-appeal well above the technical 
grade. Only 4 examples have been recorded by Kold, two of which in private ownership.
Although with the weight of a Double Ducat, this beautiful gold piece was struck with the same dies as the 1693 Mark 
(Hede-107B; KM-424.2) and was clearly not intended for regular circulation but rather for the king’s personal use.
€20,000-€25,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Joh. G. Guildal Collection (Private Sale - 1918); later published by the Danish Numismatic Society as item 1983 in his collec-
tion.

========== PAGE 67 ==========

 Bid Online at www.StacksBowers.com P age 51
Session I • Tuesday , March 24, 2026 • 12:00  PM ET / 5:00  PM CET
Superior and Highly Coveted Double Ducat
17098
DENMARK. 2 Ducat, 1693. Copenhagen Mint. Christian V . NGC MS-62. Fr-160; KM-unlisted (cf. A433, single 
Ducat); Hede-40; Sieg-107; Schou-3; Kold-135a; Bruun-7116. Weight: 6.94 gms. Mintmaster: Christian Wineke, the 
Elder. Engraver: Anton Meybusch. This exquisite Double Ducat is one of the rarest and most coveted from the whole 
Meybusch series. A surface mark is noted in the obverse left field but otherwise this is a wonderful near-Choice exem-
plar, far superior to what appears to be the only other piece in private ownership which is only VF-F .
€12,000-€16,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Frederik Chr. Bech Collection (Copenhagen - 1906) Lot # 799.
Ex: Alfred Benzon Collection (Copenhagen - 1887) Lot # 1901.
    
17099
DENMARK. Ducat, 1693. Copenhagen Mint. Christian V . NGC MS-64. Fr-156; KM-415.3; Hede-31C; Sieg-104.3; Schou-6; Bru-
un-7118. Weight: 3.46 gms. Mintmaster: Christian Wineke, the Elder (CW). With excellent surfaces, satiny devices and a completely 
even flan this near-Gem rarity is hard to overlook.
Although the engraver seems to have sought inspiration in the portraiture of the contemporary Ducats by Anton Meybusch, the present die 
is clearly from the hand of a less skilled engraver who has remained anonymous. 
€9,000-€11,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
Ex: Peter v. Hemmert Collection (Copenhagen - 1807) Lot # 155.

17100
DENMARK. 3 Krone, 1693. Copenhagen Mint. Christian 
V . NGC Unc Details—Obverse Tooled. Dav-3646; KM-430; 
Hede-101; Sieg-53; Schou-23; Kold-126d; Bruun-7120. Weight: 
54.43 gms. Mintmaster: Christian Wineke, the Elder. Engraver: 
Anton Meybusch. Some tooling in the lower right obverse field, 
but otherwise an enormously attractive exemplar with tons of 
luster and a very attractive, iridescent toning in the peripheries. 
Very rare and massive Triple Krone. 
€8,000-€10,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17101
DENMARK. Silver Pattern Krone, 1693. Copenhagen Mint. 
Christian V . NGC MS-65. Dav-3648A; KM-428.2; Hede-99B; 
Sieg-51.2; Schou-32; Kold-128; Bruun-7126. Weight: 17.85 
gms. Moneyer: Christian Wineke, the Elder. Engraver: Anton 
Meybusch (AMB). Excessively rare presentation piece / pattern 
featuring a magnificent rainbow toning to further enhance the 
wonderfully lustrous Gem condition.
The famous engraver, Anton Meybusch, who rather unusually 
signed this coin below the bust of the king, had previously worked 
in Sweden, France, Germany and England, when in 1692 he ac -
cepted a position at the Royal Mint in Copenhagen where he re -
mained until his death in 1702. 
€3,000-€4,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

131e; Bruun-7132. Moneyer: Christian Wineke, the Elder. En-
graver: Anton Meybusch. A ravishing example of the extremely 
rare type without denomination on the reverse. 
€2,000-€3,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

Only One Outside the Danish National Museum
17104
DENMARK. 2 Ducat, 1696. Copenhagen Mint. NGC AU-50. Fr-160; KM-434; Hede-42; Sieg-69; Schou-3; Kold-
138b; Bruun-7204. Weight: 6.87 gms. Mintmaster: Christian Wineke, the Elder. Engraver: Anton Meybusch. Struck 
with dies befitting a Half Ducat, this amusing two-year type is remarkably thick. Only a very few pieces of the type are 
known to be privately held, and of this date no other examples than the one in the Danish National Museum have been 
recorded by Kold.

Virtually as Struck, Very Rare and Interesting
17105
NORW AY . Gold Issue, ND (1670-80). Christiania Mint. Christian V . NGC Unc Details—Obverse Rim Filed. Fr-14; 
KM-Unlisted; Hede-20B; Sieg-Unlisted; NMD-24A; JS-516; Schou-5; Bruun-7235. Weight: 7.29 gms. As this enigmatic 
equestrian medallic type also exists in silver struck from the same dies, a test cut on the obverse rim comes as no sur-
prise. This aside, the piece is virtually as struck, presenting details normally never seen. Most interesting and excessively 
rare with no more than a handful in existence today.

From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

€5,000-€7,000

Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
HERMAN HENRIK JULIUS LYNGE  
(1822-1897) 
Herman Henrik Julius Lynge (1822-1897) is widely consid-
ered to be the father of the professional antiquarian book trade 
in Denmark. He obtained both the Danish and the Swedish 
medal of honor, and when he turned 70, he was awarded the 
title of "Kancelliråd" (titulary chancellor). His estate auction 
took place in 1899 and was divided into 5 small catalogs com-
prising a total of several thousand coins, medals, tokens and 
paper money.


========== PAGE 72 ==========

Page 56 Stack’s Bowers Galleries
The L.  E. Bruun Co llection • A Corpus of Scandinavian Monetary History Part IV
Near Choice Mint State with Exquisite Details
Iconic and Very Rare City View Ducat
17108
DENMARK. Ducat, ND (1699). Copenhagen Mint. Christian V . NGC MS-62. Fr-205a; KM-455; Hede-57; Sieg-115; 
Schou-27; Bruun-7244. Weight: 3.46 gms. A near-Choice exemplar of an iconic Scandinavian city view type of which 
there are only very few in private ownership.
Underneath the text “Hafnia Dania” (Copenhagen - Denmark) in a banner is shown a magnificent view of the capital seen 
from the harbor, from which it clearly emerges that it was not without reason that Copenhagen was dubbed, “City of Tow-
ers”. The amusing obverse with the personification of the winds blowing from the clouds over a monogrammed pyramid (no 
base type), bears the king’s motto: PIETATE ET JUSTITIA (Piety and Justice).
€12,000-€15,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: H. H. J. Lynge Collection (Copenhagen - 1899) Lot # 1814.
Ex: Alfred Benzon Collection (Copenhagen - 1887) Lot # 2051.

Photo Enlarged 2x
17109
DENMARK. 1/2 Ducat, ND (1696). Copenhagen Mint. Christian V . NGC MS-64+. Fr-183; KM-442; Hede-51; Sieg-80; JS-512; 
Schou-38; Bruun-7263. Weight: 1.83 gms. Mintmaster: Christian Wineke, the Elder. A superlative strike has left matte devices and a 
stupendous luster worthy of a Gem designation. This elegant equestrian type was struck with the same dies as the Ducat but is infinitely 
rarer as a Half Ducat. 
€5,000-€7,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
    
17110
DENMARK. Speciedaler, ND (1670). Copenhagen Mint. Christian V . NGC MS-60. Dav-3629; KM-322; Hede-61B; Sieg-58.2; 
Schou-43; Bruun-7264. Weight: 28.33 gms. Mintmaster: Gotfred Krüger. Engraver: Jeremias Hercules. Pleasantly toned and superbly 
detailed piece of the excessively rare type with a plain edge. 
This is the first regular Speciedaler of Christian V which, although not bearing a date, can be assigned to 1670 as the obverse was also used 
for the succession type, Dav-3628. 
€6,000-€8,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17111
NORW AY . Token issue, ND (1670s). Christiania Mint. Christian V . NGC MS-63. KM-unlisted; Hede-20B; Sieg-82.2; NMD-24B; 
JS-516; Schou-5a; Bruun-7270. Weight: 5.62 gms. An immensely attractive piece with superb details.
This beautiful silver issue, which was earlier believed to be Danish, has traditionally been referred to as an off-strike of a Gold coin but the 
many varying weights of both the gold and the silver pieces indicate that it should be considered a medallic issue. 
€700-€900
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

========== PAGE 74 ==========

Page 58 Stack’s Bowers Galleries
The L.  E. Bruun Co llection • A Corpus of Scandinavian Monetary History Part IV
    
17113
DENMARK. 6 Mark (Rejsedaler), 1704. Copenhagen Mint. Frederik IV . NGC MS-62. Dav-1289; KM-479.2; NMD-1A; Hede-38; 
Sieg-23.2 (Denmark), 14.2 (Norway); Schou-8; Bruun-7334. Weight: 27.05 gms. Mintmaster: Christian Wineke, the Y ounger. A sharply 
struck example with a charming, lightly mottled patina and a stupendous luster. Struck for the king’s travel to Norway which lasted 
from the 29th of May until the 5th of September 1704. 
The reverse bears an inscription by the famous Danish hymn writer, Thomas Kingo: MOD, TROSKAB, DAPPERHED OG HVAD DER 
GIVER AERE DEN HEELE VERDEN KAND BLANDT NORSKE KLIPPER LAERE (Courage, fidelity, bravery and what else brings 
honor can be learnt among the cliffs of Norway). The tradition of striking so-called, “Travel-Talers” in relation to the official royal travels to 
Norway was continued by the next three kings, Christian VI, Frederik V and Christian VII. 
€5,000-€7,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922)
Gem MS-65 and the Best in Private Ownership
17112
DENMARK. Ducat, 1700. Copenhagen Mint. Frederik IV . NGC MS-65. Fr-230; KM-471; Hede-5; Sieg-39; Schou-3; 
Kold-150b; Bruun-7300. Weight: 3.47 gms. Mintmaster: Christian Wineke, the Elder. Engraver: Anton Meybusch. A 
rare type by the talented engraver Meybusch in a spectacular Gem MS-65 condition which is surely the best in private 
ownership.
€10,000-€12,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

========== PAGE 75 ==========

 Bid Online at www.StacksBowers.com P age 59
Session I • Tuesday , March 24, 2026 • 12:00  PM ET / 5:00  PM CET
Choice Double Ducat of Extreme Rarity
17114
DENMARK. 2 Ducat, 1709. Copenhagen Mint. Frederik IV . NGC MS-63. Fr-211; KM-486; Hede-15; Sieg-44; 
Schou-1; Bruun-7358. Weight: 6.89 gms. Mintmaster: Christian Wineke, the Y ounger. With its superlative luster and 
refined details this Choice treasure is by far the best of the two coins of the type (1708 and 1709) in private ownership 
so far recorded.
Friedberg, Krause, Hede, and Sieg all describe this elegant Double Ducat with an alternating pattern of crowned arms and 
monograms to be Unique, but a second piece is in the Danish National Museum.
€20,000-€25,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Peter v. Hemmert Collection (Copenhagen - 1807) Lot # 225.

========== PAGE 76 ==========

Page 60 Stack’s Bowers Galleries
The L.  E. Bruun Co llection • A Corpus of Scandinavian Monetary History Part IV
Absolutely Stunning and Scarce Rendsborg One-Y ear Type
17115
DENMARK. Ducat, 1719. Rendsborg Mint. Frederik IV . NGC MS-62. Fr-226; KM-8; Hede-60; Sieg-61; Schou-1; 
Lange-100; Bruun-7481. Weight: 3.43 gms. Mintmaster: Bastian Hille (BH). Engraver: Gottfried Metelles. Superior 
example of this scarce one-year type minted in the fortified city of Rendsburg (Rendsborg). An absolutely stunning 
near-Choice exemplar of this two-year type, which, with only a very few in private ownership, is considerably rarer than 
the Half Ducat.
The engraver, Gottfried Wilhelm Metelles, was also active in Minden and Königsberg.
Although Rendsburg’s importance for the Danish king grew during the 18th century up until the Danish defeat in the Sec-
ond Schleswig War in 1864, the Mint was only functional for four years during the reign of Frederik IV .
€16,000-€20,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Frederik Chr. Bech Collection (Copenhagen - 1906) Lot # 942.
Ex: L. Christian Nielsen Collection (Copenhagen - 1878) Lot # 79.

========== PAGE 77 ==========

 Bid Online at www.StacksBowers.com P age 61
Session I • Tuesday , March 24, 2026 • 12:00  PM ET / 5:00  PM CET
    
  
Photo Enlarged 2x
17116
DENMARK. Skilling, 1719. Rendsborg Mint. Frederik IV . NGC MS-67. KM-5; Hede-63; Sieg-58.2; Schou-12; Bruun-7488. Mint-
master: Bastian Hille (BH). Engraver: Gottfried Metelles. A “non plus ultra” example with a ‘must see to believe’ luster.
€500-€800
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Outstanding and Highly Coveted Pattern
17117
DENMARK. Silver Pattern 12 Skilling, 1721. Copenhagen Mint. Frederik IV . NGC MS-66. KM-522; Hede-50; Sieg-
13; Schou-1; Bruun-7506. Mintmaster: Christian Wineke, the Y ounger (CW). A perfectly struck presentation piece 
with a wonderful steely grey toning over a compelling silky luster. Not more than a handful in total is known of this 
interesting pattern which easily ranks among the most coveted types from the reign of Frederik IV . 
The only other example to have appeared on the market, passing through the collections of prominent Danish collectors 
like K. Kold (2020), Chr. Zinck (2003), S. V . Broge (1938) and H. H. Schou (1927), is only Very Fine and couldn’t hold a 
candle to the present.
€15,000-€20,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Joh. G. Guildal Collection (Private Sale - 1918); later published by the Danish Numismatic Society as item 2322 in his collec-
tion.

========== PAGE 78 ==========

Page 62 Stack’s Bowers Galleries
The L.  E. Bruun Co llection • A Corpus of Scandinavian Monetary History Part IV
Johan Peter Samuel Goldschmidt, who as a coin collector is best known by 
the surname Guildal, which he acquired in 1906, was born in Copenhagen in 
1855, the son of a shipbroker. After completing an apprenticeship in a whole-
saler’s company, he traveled to both England and Germany to study dyeing 
technology, so that on his return he was qualified to enter his uncle’s dye -
ing company, Goldschmidt and Nordholm. Together with the Swede Anders 
Nordholm, Johan Guildal took over the management as early as 1880; when 
the company changed to a limited company in 1906, he continued as director 
until 1919. At the end of the 19th 
century and the beginning of the 
20th, Guildal was a central figure 
within Danish industry and crafts-
manship and held board positions 
in many important companies 
such as Handelsbanken, The Dan-
ish Coal Company, Danish Lloyd, 
The Danish-Russian Steamship 
Company, Atlas Machine Factory 
and Portland Cement Factory.
In 1899, the same year he was awarded the Order of the Dannebrog, Guil-
dal participated in the nine-month long voyage to East Asia on board the 
protected cruiser, Valkyrien (“The Valkyrie”), under the command of the 
king’s youngest son, Prince Valdemar.  The trip had been advocated ex-
pressly by the Danish parliament at the urging of Danish business inter -
ests. On the way visits were made to both the Siamese king Chulalongkorn 
and the Japanese emperor Meiji.
Guildal was also a prominent art collector, and his collection of old master prints was sold by Winkel & Magnus -
sen in 1931. Prints from his collection (easily recognizable from the small ink stamp verso, Lugt 1110a) can now be 
found in museums all over the world, including at the Metropolitan Museum in New Y ork and the Rijksmuseum in 
Amsterdam.
His main passion, however, was his coin collection. He was one of the founding fathers of the first Danish society for 
coin collectors formed in 1885, where L. E. Bruun was also a member. From 1914 through 1916 he acted as chair -
man of the still existing Numismatisk Forening (Danish Numismatic Society). Unlike L. E. Bruun, he didn’t collect 
medieval coins, but instead focused on the issues struck by the House of Oldenburg, which ruled Denmark from 
1448 to 1863.
Given his interest in the graphic arts it was only natural that he also collected paper money. Whereas this collection 
of 562 pieces is today a part of the collection at the National Museum of Denmark, Guildal decided to sell his coin 
collection in its entirety to L. E. Bruun in June 1918. Guildal was not a stranger to the idea of trading large collections 
en bloc, as he had himself acquired all Major General Carl Thorvald Jørgensen’s (1819-1902) formidable collection 
of Danish and Norwegian coins in 1901.
Shortly before his death on December 21, 1920, Guildal’s collection was published as vol. III of “Numismatisk 
Forenings Medlemsblad” and cataloged by Hans Henrik Schou. 
J. G. GUILDAL
(1850-1920) 
J.G. Guildal, Danish Royal Library.
Royal Danish Order of the 
Dannebrog, Grand Cross Star.

========== PAGE 79 ==========

 Bid Online at www.StacksBowers.com P age 63
Session I • Tuesday , March 24, 2026 • 12:00  PM ET / 5:00  PM CET
    
17118
DENMARK. Ducat, 1725. Copenhagen Mint. Frederik IV . 
NGC MS-62. Fr-244; KM-484; Hede-11B; Sieg-42.2; Schou-1; 
Bruun-7522. Weight: 3.42 gms. Mintmaster: Christian Wineke, 
the Y ounger. A near Choice Uncirculated example with a re-
markable luster and excellent details; probably the best in private 
hands.
A very rare type struck from African gold. The reverse shows the 
Danish outpost on Africa’s Gold Coast, Fort Christiansborg (now 
Osu Castle), located in what is now Ghana along the Gulf of Guin-
ea. The fortress became the Danish administrative headquarters 
of Guinea in 1685 and was predominantly used as a place of em-
barkation of enslaved local captives for the Danish colonies in the 
Caribbean. 
€8,000-€10,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
    
  
Photo Enlarged 2x
17119
DENMARK. 2 Ducat, ND (before 1710). Copenhagen Mint. 
Frederik IV . NGC Unc Details—Cleaned. Fr-239; KM-499; 
Hede-24; Sieg-31; JS-676; Schou-3; Bruun-7542. Weight: 6.95 
gms. Mintmaster: Christian Wineke, the Y ounger. This enigmat-
ic, elegant and excessively rare equestrian type was struck in both 
gold (with the weight of 2, 1 and 1/2 Ducat) and silver. The hefty 
Double Ducat, which is the largest of the series, has not been on 
the market for ages. 
€4,000-€6,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
Ex: Peter v. Hemmert Collection (Copenhagen - 1807) Lot # 251.
    
17120
DENMARK. 4 Mark (Krone), 1731. Copenhagen Mint. Chris-
tian VI. NGC MS-64. Dav-1294; KM-537; Hede-4; Sieg-5.2; 
Schou-2; Bruun-7565. Weight: 21.85 gms. Mintmaster: Christian 
Wineke, the Y ounger (CW). Engraver: Georg Wilhelm Wahl. A 
wonderfully lustrous and unusually well-struck near-Gem of the 
“large crown” type. 
€3,000-€4,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17121
DENMARK. Ducat, 1740. Copenhagen Mint. Christian VI. 
NGC MS-62. Fr-248; KM-542; Hede-3; Sieg-9; Schou-1; Bru-
un-7586. Weight: 3.48 gms. Mintmaster: Christian Wineke, the 
Y ounger. A spectacular near-Choice piece of this historically in-
teresting type.
At the time a stronghold for the Danish Atlantic slave trade, the 
fortress Christiansborg still stands and was until 2008 the presi-
dential palace of Ghana. 
€6,000-€8,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
Ex: Peter v. Hemmert Collection (Copenhagen - 1807) Lot # 269.
    
  
Photo Enlarged 2x
17122
DENMARK. Skilling, 1746. Copenhagen Mint. Christian VI. 
NGC MS-66. KM-541; Hede-8; Sieg-2; Schou-3; Bruun-7600. 
Mintmaster: Christian Wineke, the Y ounger (CW). Engraver: Jo-
hann Heinrich Wahl. Only three minor coins were struck during 
the reign of Christian VI: the 1/2 Skilling 1745 and the 1 Skilling 
1735 and 1746, meaning that these saw a lot of circulation. Find-
ing an example like this in a Gem MS-66 simply defies logic. 
€800-€1,200
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

The L.  E. Bruun Co llection • A Corpus of Scandinavian Monetary History Part IV
Extremely Rare Double Ducat with Radiant Luster
17124
DENMARK. 2 Ducat, 1747. Copenhagen Mint. Frederik V . NGC MS-63. Fr-260; KM-568.2; Hede-10B; Sieg-31.2; 
Schou-6; Bruun-7615. Weight: 6.94 gms. Mintmaster: Ingeborg Maria Wineke. Engraver: Magnus Gustavus Arbien (A). 
Finely detailed matte-like devices contrast brilliantly with the radiant luster in the fields of this extremely rare Double 
Ducat.
€8,000-€10,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17123
DENMARK. 3 Krone, ND (1746). Copenhagen Mint. Frederik V . NGC Unc Details—Cleaned. Dav-1297; KM-546; Hede-28; Sieg-
14; Schou-3; Bruun-7603. Weight: 45.35 gms. Mintmaster: Ingeborg Maria Wineke. Engraver: Peter Christian Winsløw (W). Although 
once upon a time lightly cleaned, this lustrous and highly detailed piece towers well above what one normally sees.
This beautiful Triple Krone was struck in only 500 specimens on the occasion of the succession and bears the elegant edge inscription: GIVE 
GUD TIL VERDENS ENDE, SLIGE LYS I NORDEN BRENDE (“May God let such light shine in the North to the ends of the earth”). 
€4,000-€6,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

Stellar Equestrian Double Ducat
Presumably Unique in Private Hands
17125
DENMARK. 2 Ducat, 1748. Copenhagen Mint. Frederik V . NGC MS-66+ H. Fr-276; KM-574; Hede-16; Sieg-37; 
Schou-1; Bruun-7628. Weight: 6.92 gms. Mintmaster: Ingeborg Maria Wineke. The wonderful deep strike and proof-
like appearance elevates this Gem-66+H to a level of eye-appeal that most certainly deserves its star designation. With 
presumably the only other Double Ducat of this type resting safely in the Danish National Museum this is as not to be 
missed chance of acquiring a truly stellar piece from the reign of Frederik V .
€10,000-€12,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

========== PAGE 82 ==========

Page 66 Stack’s Bowers Galleries
The L.  E. Bruun Co llection • A Corpus of Scandinavian Monetary History Part IV
Spectacularly lustrous Double Ducat of the Utmost Rarity
Struck for The Danish West India Company
17126
DENMARK. 2 Ducat, 1749. Copenhagen Mint. Frederik V . NGC MS-63. Fr-274; KM-unlisted; Hede-18; Sieg-39; 
Schou-1; Bruun-7638. Weight: 6.95 gms. Mintmaster: Peter Nicolai van Hauen (VH). Engraver: Magnus Gustavus 
Arbien. A spectacularly lustrous Choice Mint State Double Ducat of the utmost rarity with no more than a handful 
examples recorded.
Below the trident, symbol of Poseidon / Neptune, the banner bears the initials of the Danish West Indian Company, and 
this wonderful equestrian type with the asymmetrical arms so typical for the Rococo period, is thus a key type for collectors 
of the West Indies.
€20,000-€25,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Frederik Chr. Bech Collection (Copenhagen - 1906) Lot # 1044.
Ex: Alfred Benzon Collection (Copenhagen - 1887) Lot # 2463.

Weight: 3.49 gms. Mintmaster: Peter Nicolai van Hauen (VH). Engraver: Magnus Gustavus Arbien. An absolutely stunning Near-Gem 
uncirculated equestrian Ducat. 
€6,000-€8,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

17129
DENMARK. Skilling, 1751. Copenhagen Mint. Frederik V .

un-7648. Mintmaster: Peter Nicolai Van Hauen (VH). Engraver:

that the silver content is less than 20%. Fully lustrous and lightly 
toned example with all details clearly struck.

Breathtakingly Beautiful Prooflike Maritime Ducat
17130
DENMARK. Ducat, 1753. Copenhagen Mint. Frederik V . NGC MS-67 Prooflike. Fr-268; KM-583; Hede-19; Sieg-40; 
Schou-2; Bruun-7651. Weight: 3.49 gms. Mintmaster: Peter Nicolai van Hauen (VH); Engraver: Magnus Gustavus Ar-
bien. With its lovely matte devices and its breathtaking luster, this wonderfully pristine exemplar of a scarce date seems 
completely untouched by time and undoubtedly ranks as the finest in private hands of all three dates issued.
Bearing a symbolic reverse, the ship design on this type designates that the gold used to coin this piece was acquired from 
overseas. The Latin legend, “DUCE PRUDENTIA, CONSTANTIA COMITE”, which has roots in religious teachings, can 
be translated as “With Wisdom as a Guide, Perseverance as a Companion”.
€20,000-€25,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

========== PAGE 84 ==========

Page 68 Stack’s Bowers Galleries
The L.  E. Bruun Co llection • A Corpus of Scandinavian Monetary History Part IV
    
  
Photo Enlarged 2x
17131
DENMARK. Ducat, 1754. Copenhagen Mint. Frederik V . NGC MS-66 Prooflike. Fr-268; KM-583; Hede-19; Sieg-40; Schou-1; 
Bruun-7653. Weight: 3.50 gms. Mintmaster: Peter Nicolai van Hauen (VH). Engraver: Magnus Gustavus Arbien. The technical grade 
alone should say it all, but when holding the piece in your hand you can almost feel the wind in your hair. A maritime Ducat from the 
very first tier.
Bearing a symbolic reverse, the ship design on this type designates that the gold used to coin this piece was acquired from overseas. The Latin 
legend, “DUCE PRUDENTIA, CONSTANTIA COMITE”, which has roots in religious teachings, can be translated as “With Wisdom as a 
Guide, Perseverance as a Companion”. 
€12,000-€15,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Vilhelm Bergsøe Collection (Copenhagen 1890) Lot # 345.
    
  
Photo Enlarged 2x
17132
DENMARK. 1/2 Skilling, 1755. Copenhagen Mint. Frederik V . NGC MS-65 Brown. KM-577.1; Hede-40A; Sieg-1.1; Schou-4; Bru-
un-7656. Mintmaster: Peter Nicolai Van Hauen (PNVH). Engraver: Georg Wilhelm Wahl. Flaming red luster hugs the design, with 
sharp details throughout.
1/2 Skilling 1751 and 1755 were issued at the request of the governors of the poor houses, as a denomination smaller than 1 Skilling was 
needed for the church collection plates. The coins were recalled and demonetized in 1775. 
€800-€1,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

========== PAGE 85 ==========

 Bid Online at www.StacksBowers.com P age 69
Session I • Tuesday , March 24, 2026 • 12:00  PM ET / 5:00  PM CET
Stunning Gem Ducat Struck From Norwegian Gold
17133
DENMARK. Ducat, 1758. Copenhagen Mint. Frederik V . NGC MS-66. Fr-261; KM-588; Hede-21; Sieg-42; Schou-1; 
Bruun-7667. Weight: 3.48 gms. Mintmaster: Peter Nicolai van Hauen (VH); Engraver: Magnus Gustavus Arbien (A). 
An absolutely stunning Gem of this beautiful one-year type which is presumed to be the only Danish Ducat struck from 
gold mined in Norway. Excessively rare in such a wonderful condition.
Of the original mintage of 1,001 coins, 700 were immediately re-issued as Ducat Courants. With a gold content of c. 20% 
less, these were both more practical for circulation and more profitable for the king. The elegant, allegorical reverse design 
shows the Danish arms resting on the Eben-Ezer from the Books of Samuel.
€8,000-€12,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Frederik Chr. Bech Collection (Copenhagen - 1906) Lot # 1066.

========== PAGE 86 ==========

Page 70 Stack’s Bowers Galleries
The L.  E. Bruun Co llection • A Corpus of Scandinavian Monetary History Part IV
    
  
Photo Enlarged 2x
17134
DENMARK. 12 Mark (Courant Ducat), 1758. Copenhagen 
Mint. Frederik V . NGC MS-65+. Fr-269; KM-587.2; Hede-22B; 
Sieg-21.2; Schou-4; Bruun-7668. Weight: 3.11 gms. Mintmas -
ter: Peter Nicolai van Hauen (VH). Engraver: Magnus Gustavus 
Arbien (A). An immensely attractive exemplar of this gold type 
which - as opposed to practically all other Ducats - was actually 
struck for circulation, making it almost impossible to find in a 
sublime Gem condition like the present.
In all other dies of the whole series (1757-1765) the king’s portrait 
is bare but is here shown with a light drapery around his neck. 
€4,000-€6,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
  
Photo Enlarged 2x
17135
DENMARK. 8 Skilling, 1763. Copenhagen Mint. Frederik V . 
NGC MS-64+. KM-596; Hede-38B; Sieg-5.2; Schou-15; Bru -
un-7703. Mintmaster: Hans Schierven Knoph (HSK). Engraver: 
Johan Ephraim Bauert. Single-year type, monogram ‘F5’ . Highly 
lustrous with old toning. 
Three variations of the monogram exist for this date, with the pres-
ent being the rarest. 
€700-€1,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17136
DENMARK. Speciedaler, 1764. Copenhagen Mint. Frederik 
V . NGC MS-64. Dav-1302A; KM-599; Hede-27A; Sieg-18.1; 
Schou-1; Bruun-7711. Weight: 28.62 gms. Mintmaster: Hans 
Schierven Knoph (HSK). Engraver: Johan Ephraim Bauert (B). 
A highly attractive near-Gem with amazing surfaces and a deli-
cate toning with speckles of turquoise.
The very characteristic portrait which bears reminiscence to J. J. 
Saly’s equestrian statue at Amalienborg Castle was created by the 
Swedish born engraver, J. E. Bauert who also provided dies for the 
production at Norway and Oldenburg. 
€8,000-€12,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17137
DENMARK. 24 Skilling, 1767. Copenhagen Mint. Christian 
VII. NGC MS-65. KM-602; Hede-11; Sieg-27; Schou-2; FP-E2; 
Bruun-7735. Weight: 9.15 gms. Mintmaster: Hans Schierven 
Knoph (HSK). Engraver: Johan Ephraim Bauert. A scarce type in 
an almost unbelievable Gem condition with perfect details and 
exceptional, velvety brilliance.
Struck for the king’s anointment on May 1st 1767 in a number of 
3,200 to be used as largesse money at the Riding Grounds at Chris-
tiansborg Castle in Copenhagen. 
€4,000-€5,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

========== PAGE 87 ==========

 Bid Online at www.StacksBowers.com P age 71
Session I • Tuesday , March 24, 2026 • 12:00  PM ET / 5:00  PM CET
    
17138
DENMARK. Speciedaler, 1769. Rethwisch Mint. Christian VII. NGC MS-65. Dav-1306; KM-608; Hede-8; Sieg-36.1; Schou-5; FP-
R4.2; Bruun-7750. Weight: 28.89 gms. Mintmaster: Hans Schievern Knoph (HSK). Engraver: Johan Ephraim Bauert. Although not a 
rare type, it is very difficult to find in nice condition, and a spectacular Gem like the present is practically unheard-of.
Rethwisch castle was located between Lübeck and Hamburg in Holstein. The Rethwisch Mint, a subsidiary to the Royal Mint in Copenha-
gen, operated for only three years, 1768-1770, when it was moved to Altona. The coins minted at Rethwisch are considered among the most 
beautiful of Danish coins. 
€3,000-€4,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17139
DENMARK. 1/2 Speciedaler, 1769. Rethwisch Mint. Christian VII. NGC MS-67. KM-605; Hede-9B; Sieg-32; Schou-10; FP-R2.1; 
Lange- ; Bruun-7753. Weight: 14.41 gms. Mintmaster: Hans Schierven Knoph (HSK). Engraver: Johan Ephraim Bauert. Blazing luster 
and superbly frosty devices combine to make this example an unparalleled Superb Gem Uncirculated. Scarce variation without de -
nomination in a condition which will probably never be seen again. 
€7,000-€10,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

========== PAGE 88 ==========

Page 72 Stack’s Bowers Galleries
The L.  E. Bruun Co llection • A Corpus of Scandinavian Monetary History Part IV
Highly Lustrous Exemplar with Immense Eye-Appeal
Intensely Coveted “Greenland” Trade Dollar
17140
DENMARK. Piaster, 1771 (struck in 1774). Copenhagen Mint. Christian VII. NGC MS-62. Dav-411A; KM-638; 
Hede-21; Sieg-17; Schou-6; FP-32; Salvesen-51 (small crown type); Bruun-7759. Weight: 26.80 gms. Mintmaster: Hans 
Schievern Knoph. Engraver: Johan Ephraim Bauert. With its subtle toning, matte devices, and a blazing luster, this 
example presents an amazing eye-appeal and is bound to become a highlight in any collection of 18th century crowns. 
Very rare.
This iconic one-year type was struck by the Danish East Asia Company for the Far East trade modeled on the dominant 
Trade Dollar of the time, the Spanish Colonial Eight Reales. Internationally it is often - somewhat misleadingly - referred 
to as the “Greenland Dollar” because of the inscription above the date, mentioning the Danish Arctic possessions, Iceland, 
Greenland, Faroe Islands (“ISLAND, GRÖNLAN, FERÖ”).
€60,000-€80,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

17146
DENMARK. Ducat, 1792. Altona Mint. Christian VII. NGC 
MS-63+. Fr-283; KM-650; Hede-2; Sieg-29; Schou-1; FP-47.3; 
Bruun-7912. Weight: 3.49 gms. Mintmaster: Michael Flor. En-
graver: Georg Valentin Bauert. A very handsome Choice+ ex -
ample displaying a rich luster and sharply rendered design ele -
ments.
The so-called “Wildman Ducat” was the last Danish Ducat pro -
duced and the only one which gives the exact weight and fineness 
in the design, suiting it for international trade.

From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

17147
DENMARK. 1/15 Speciedaler, 1799. Altona Mint. Christian 
VII. NGC MS-66. KM-652; Hede-15; Sieg-20; Schou-11; Bru -
un-7951. Mintmaster: Michael Flor (MF). Engraver: Georg Val-
entin Bauert. Premium Gem Uncirculated example.
€800-€1,200
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17148
DENMARK. Silver Pattern 6 Skilling, 1812. Copenhagen 
Mint. Frederik VI. NGC PROOF-63. KM-PnA52; Hede-12; 
Sieg-40 (copper); Schou-4a; Bruun-8016. Weight: 4.16 gms. 
Mintmaster: Ole Varberg. Engraver: Johannes Conradsen (IC). 
Perfectly struck, lustrous and with a fine, blue toning. This ex-
cessively rare silver strike should be considered a presentation 
piece of a pattern which is normally only seen in copper as the 
intended 6 Skilling.
The elegantly engrailed edge proves that this is a strictly contempo-
rary pattern and not a restrike. The pattern was rejected as it was 
too similar to the circulating 2 Skillings (Hede-9). 
€3,000-€4,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Handsome Proof Pattern of Which Only Two are Known
17149
DENMARK. Copper Pattern 4 Skilling, 1812. Copenhagen Mint. Frederik VI. NGC PROOF-63 Brown. KM-Pn50; 
Hede-13; Sieg-39; Schou-5; Bruun-8019. Mintmaster: Ole Varberg. Engraver: Johannes Conradsen. A pleasing proof 
with rich eye-appeal and handsome toning. With only two examples recorded (the other one being in the National 
Museum of Denmark), this is the first time this type has been on the market for almost 140 years.
In 1812 three denominations were issued in copper, 12, 3 and 1 Skilling, whereas the projected denominations of 6 and 4 
Skilling were never realized.
€6,000-€8,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
Ex: Alfred Benzon Collection (Copenhagen - 1887) Lot # 2994.

========== PAGE 91 ==========

 Bid Online at www.StacksBowers.com P age 75
Session I • Tuesday , March 24, 2026 • 12:00  PM ET / 5:00  PM CET
    
17150
DENMARK. Rigsbankdaler, 1813. Copenhagen Mint. Freder-
ik VI. NGC MS-66. KM-683.2; Hede-24B; Sieg-26.2; Schou-2; 
Bruun-8026. Weight: 14.48 gms. Mintmaster: Ole Varberg. En-
graver: Johannes Conradsen (IC). Coruscating fields with hues 
of blue, pink and gold makes this piece a safe bet for any collector 
of quality coins. 
€1,800-€2,200
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17151
DENMARK. 12 Skilling Rigsbanktegn, 1813. Copenhagen 
Mint. Frederik VI. NGC MS-65 Red Brown. KM-Tn2; Hede-
19; Sieg-13; Schou-4; Bruun-8027. Mintmaster: Ole Varberg. 
Engraver: Conrad Frederik Gerlach. A superb example of this 
token issue struck after the bankruptcy of 1813; a couple of spots 
on otherwise astounding surfaces. 
€800-€1,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17152
DENMARK. 6 Skilling Rigsbanktegn, 1813. Copenhagen 
Mint. Frederik VI. NGC MS-66 Red Brown. KM-Tn1; Hede-
20; Sieg-12.1; Schou-6; Bruun-8028. Mintmaster: Ole Varberg. 
Engraver: Conrad Frederik Gerlach. Impossibly well-preserved 
coin with lustrous surfaces, proof-like in parts, and wonderful 
color. While this token issue type is quite common it is never 
seen in conditions even remotely close to this Gem.
This type was minted in both Copenhagen and Kongsberg, Nor -
way, with subtle differences. 
€800-€1,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17153
DENMARK. Rigsbankskilling, 1813. Copenhagen Mint. 
Frederik VI. NGC MS-65 Red Brown. KM-680; Hede-17; Sieg-
16; Schou-7; Bruun-8029. Mintmaster: Ole Varberg. Engraver: 
Johannes Conradsen (IC). Almost as struck with only a slight -
ly darker hue to the high points, lustrous, flaming red surfaces 
hinting towards proof-like. An impossible survivor of this coin, 
which was the first struck after the government declared a state 
of bankruptcy in 1813. 
€600-€800
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17154
DENMARK. 16 Skilling Rigsbanktegn, 1814. Copenhagen 
Mint. Frederik VI. NGC MS-64 Red Brown. KM-Tn3; Hede-
18; Sieg-14; Schou-1; Bruun-8031. Mintmaster: Ole Varberg. En-
graver: Conrad Frederik Gerlach. Another remarkable example 
of the token issue Skillings of 1813, this one the largest denomi-
nation. Very simplistic in its design with the large surfaces virtu-
ally untouched and with great luster and color. 
€800-€1,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17155
DENMARK. 4 Skilling Rigsbanktegn, 1815. Copenhagen 
Mint. Frederik VI. NGC MS-65 Red Brown. KM-Tn6; Hede-
21; Sieg-11; Schou-1; Bruun-8032. Mintmaster: Ole Varberg. En-
graver: Conrad Frederik Gerlach. Reflective, mirror-like surfaces 
adorn this flaming red copper coin, which is scarce in Mint State 
condition and impossibly so in Gem Mint. 
€700-€800
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

17156
DENMARK. 3 Skilling Rigsbanktegn, 1815. Copenhagen 
Mint. Frederik VI. NGC MS-64 Red Brown. KM-Tn5; Hede-
22; Sieg-10; Schou-2; Bruun-8033. Mintmaster: Ole Varberg. En-
graver: Conrad Frederik Gerlach. Scarce in this lofty condition, 
this token issue has surfaces bordering on proof-like. 
€500-€700
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
  
Photo Enlarged 2x
17157
DENMARK. 2 Skilling Rigsbanktegn, 1815. Copenhagen 
Mint. Frederik VI. NGC MS-64+ Red Brown. KM-Tn4; Hede-
23; Sieg-9; Schou-3; Bruun-8034. Mintmaster: Ole Varberg. En-
graver: Conrad Frederik Gerlach. Flashing red copper surfaces; 
the Rigsbanktegn tokens of 1813-1815 are all quite common, as 
emissions were large, however finding a Mint State example like 
this is next to impossible. 
€400-€600
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17158
DENMARK. Copper Pattern 2 Rigsbankskilling, 1818. Copen-
hagen Mint. Frederik VI. NGC MS-64 Red Brown. KM-Pn52; 
Hede-35; Sieg-41; Schou-6; Bruun-8042. Weight: 12.45gms. 
Mintmaster: Ole Varberg. Engraver: Christian Andreas Müller 
(M). A lustrous Near Gem Pattern with a nice Red Brown color. 
Of the only four examples recorded, the present and the one at 
the National Museum are in exquisite condition whereas the two 
others in private ownership are decidedly unattractive (cf. illus-
tration in Krause).
For the obverse of this pattern was reused the die cut by Christian 
Andreas Müller for the Copenhagen Rigsbankdaler 1813 (Hede-
24A). Possibly to avoid tempting weak souls to silver the 2 Rigs -
bankskillings to pass them off as Rigsbankdalers, the National Coat 
of Arms was preferred to the portrait of the king. 
€4,000-€5,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
    
  
Photo Enlarged 2x
17159
DENMARK. 8 Rigsbankskilling, 1818. Altona Mint. Frederik 
VI. NGC MS-65. KM-152; Hede-31B; Sieg-22.2; Schou-4; Bru-
un-8047. Mintmaster: Cajus Branth (CB). Engraver: Georg Val-
entin Bauert. The highest graded by NGC, with playful, bluish 
luster on almost mirror-like surfaces.  
€1,000-€1,200
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

========== PAGE 93 ==========

 Bid Online at www.StacksBowers.com P age 77
Session I • Tuesday , March 24, 2026 • 12:00  PM ET / 5:00  PM CET
    
17160
DENMARK. Speciedaler, 1819. Altona Mint. Frederik VI. NGC MS-64. Dav-72; KM-693; Hede-25; Sieg-29; Schou-1; Bruun-8048. 
Weight: 28.83 gms. Mintmaster: Johan Friedrich Freund (IFF). Engraver: Johannes Conradsen (JC). Absolutely ravishing example 
boasting an exceptional luster and a delightful toning. Uncommon and always popular one-year type which for the last time uses the 
Norwegian lion in the Danish arms, very rare in choice condition.
Although struck in Altona, the obverse portrait was engraved by the chief engraver af the Copenhagen Mint, Johannes Conradsen, who 
rather unusually for a Danish coin signed his work, JC F, using the Latin abbreviation, F (Fecit = made by). 
€4,000-€6,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17161
DENMARK. 32 Rigsbankskilling, 1820. Altona Mint. Frederik VI. NGC MS-66. KM-690.2; Hede-29B; Sieg-25.2; Schou-3; Bru-
un-8053. Weight: 6.07 gms. Mintmaster: Johan Friedrich Freund (IFF). Engraver: Georg Valentin Bauert. A tantalizing Gem with a 
proof-like appearance otherwise never encountered in this type. 
€1,800-€2,200
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17162
DENMARK. Speciedaler, 1822. Altona Mint. Frederik VI. NGC MS-64. KM-695.1; Hede-26D; Sieg-30.4; Schou-2; Bruun-8055. 
Weight: 28.88 gms. Mintmaster: Johan Friedrich Freund (FF). Engraver: Johannes Conradsen (IC). A lovely, untouched Near-Gem 
with an abundance of silky luster. 
€1,800-€2,200
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

========== PAGE 94 ==========

Page 78 Stack’s Bowers Galleries
The L.  E. Bruun Co llection • A Corpus of Scandinavian Monetary History Part IV
Beautiful, Interesting and Unique in Private Hands
17163
DENMARK. Speciedaler (Pattern), 1822. Altona Mint. Frederik VI. NGC MS-63. Dav-unlisted; KM-unlisted; Hede-
26A, note p. 194; Sieg-unlisted; Schou-5, Bruun-8057. Weight: 28.88 gms. Mintmaster: Johan Friedrich Freund (FF). 
Engravers: Johannes Conradsen (IC), Georg Valentin Bauert. A Choice, Mint State stunner with an imposing luster 
and fine multicolored toning along the rim. Historically and numismatically interesting, this Pattern issue is presum-
ably unique in private hands as the piece in the Benzon collection (Copenhagen Auction, 1888, #8063) is now in the 
National Museum. 
This Pattern reads GUD BESKYTTE DANNEMARK (“May God protect Denmark”) with reeding spacing the words on 
the edge, as distinguished from the standard circulation issues with regular edge reeding. Such issues with edge lettering, of 
which 4 variations are known, are of the highest rarity, with only a few specimens divided between the National Museum 
of Denmark, the Hermitage Museum and the Bruun collection.
€12,000-€16,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).
    
17164
DENMARK. Rigsbankdaler (1/2 Speciedaler), 1826. Altona Mint. Frederik VI. NGC MS-66. KM-696.1; Hede-27A; Sieg-27.1; 
Schou-3; Bruun-8067. Weight: 14.43 gms. Mintmaster: Johan Friedrich Freund (FF). Engraver: Hans Frederik Alsing (FA). Immacu-
lately toned and exceptionally beautiful Gem piece of Alsing’s first coin. 
€3,000-€4,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

Session I • Tuesday , March 24, 2026 • 12:00  PM ET / 5:00  PM CET
Radiating with Satiny Brilliance
17165
DENMARK. Frederik d’ or, 1827. Altona Mint. Frederik VI. NGC MS-65. Fr-285; KM-698; Hede-2; Sieg-31; Schou-2; 
Bruun-8069. Weight: 6.63 gms. Mintmaster: Johan Friedrich Freund (IFF). Engraver: Johannes Conradsen (IC). Scarce 
one-year type in an outstanding Gem Mint State example radiating with satiny brilliance.

Proof Cameo and Only a Handful Known
17168
DENMARK. Pattern 1/4 Speciedaler, 1833. Altona Mint. Frederik VI. NGC PROOF-64 Cameo. KM-Pn53; Hede-
28; Sieg-42; Schou-8; Bruun-8091. Weight: 7.28 gms. Mintmaster: Johan Friedrich Freund. Engraver: Hans Frederik 
Alsing (FA). A highly attractive specimen depicting razor sharp details, mirror-like fields and a pleasing toning. Exces-
sively rare with not much more than a handful of examples known.
This one-year type was struck as a pattern for a never realized denomination, a 1/4 Speciedaler or “En halv Rigsbankdaler”. 
In 1854 and 1855, during the reign of Frederik VII, a coin of the exact same weight and silver content was issued, now bear-
ing the denomination “1/2 Rigsdaler”.
€10,000-€14,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

========== PAGE 97 ==========

 Bid Online at www.StacksBowers.com P age 81
Session I • Tuesday , March 24, 2026 • 12:00  PM ET / 5:00  PM CET
    
17169
DENMARK. Rigsbankdaler, 1834. Copenhagen Mint. Freder-
ik VI. NGC MS-63 Prooflike. KM-706.2; Hede-27B; Sieg-28.2; 
Schou-6; Bruun-8093. Weight: 14.35 gms. Engraver: Hans 
Frederik Alsing (FA). Uncommon date and very difficult to find 
in the highest grades. Some light contact marks on the proof-like 
surfaces and a blue tinted toning spreading from the devices.
The mintmaster had been arrested on suspicion of fraud and so his 
initials were replaced with ‘KM’ (Københavns Mønt / Copenhagen 
Mint) in 1833 and 1834. 
€2,000-€2,500
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17170
DENMARK. Speciedaler, 1835. Copenhagen Mint. Frederik 
VI. NGC PROOF-63 Cameo. Dav-73; KM-695.4; Hede-26C; 
Sieg-30.3; Schou-3; Bruun-8098. Weight: 28.95 gms. Mintmas-
ter: Georg Wilhelm Svendsen (WS). Engraver: Johannes Con -
radsen (IC). True proof-strikes with full cameo appearance are 
very rare, so even though a few surface marks can be seen in the 
fields, this wonderfully blue-toned piece is of the utmost desir -
ability. 
€2,000-€2,400
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17171
DENMARK. Rigsbankdaler (1/2 Speciedaler), 1835. Altona

Johan Friedrich Freund. Engraver: Hans Frederik Alsing (FA). 
A subtle rainbow toning and a most impressive, silky sheen 
combine to make this superlative Gem Mint State example un -
improvable. Very rare date.

The L.  E. Bruun Co llection • A Corpus of Scandinavian Monetary History Part IV
Near Choice Pattern. Presumably Unique in Private Hands
17174
DENMARK. Silver Pattern (without denomination), ND (1809-12). Copenhagen Mint. Frederik VI. NGC MS-62. 
KM-Not listed; Hede-Not listed; Sieg-Not listed; Schou-2; Bruun-8129. Weight: 4.75 gms. Mintmaster: Hans Jacob Ar-
nold Branth. Engraver: Johannes Conradsen (IC). Near Choice, eminently lustrous and pleasingly toned. Only recorded 
in two examples, the present and the one in the National Museum (acquired at the 1867 Devegge sale).
This highly interesting type without date and denomination has been overlooked by almost all reference catalogs. Based 
on the weight it could be considered a pattern for an unrealized 1/6 Speciedaler as an equivalent to the equally unrealized 
Christian VII patterns (Hede-19, Sieg-47) which similarly carried neither date nor denomination.
€6,000-€8,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17175
DENMARK. Speciedaler, 1840. Altona Mint. Christian VIII. NGC MS-65 Prooflike. Dav-74; KM-720.2; Hede-3B; Sieg-13.2; 
Schou-2; Bruun-8133. Weight: 28.91 gms. Mintmaster: Johan Friedrich Freund (FF). Engraver: Johannes Conradsen. NGC TOP POP . 
A full Gem with delightful cabinet tone over mirrored fields on both sides and splendidly crisp detailing. 
€3,500-€5,500
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

17177
DENMARK. 16 Rigsbankskilling, 1842. Copenhagen Mint. 
Christian VIII. NGC MS-65. KM-733; Hede-6; Sieg-10; 
Schou-4; Bruun-8140. Weight: 4.21 gms. Mintmaster: Georg

€700-€1,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17178
DENMARK. 2 Rigsbankskilling, 1842. Copenhagen Mint. 
Christian VIII. NGC MS-64 Red Brown. KM-728; Hede-
10; Sieg-5; Schou-9; Bruun-8142. Mintmaster: Georg Vilhelm 
Svendsen (VS). Engraver: Frederik Christopher Krohn (FK). 
NGC TOP POP; a beautiful and always popular large-diameter 
single-year type with lustrous, shiny surfaces and some slightly 
darker toning which brings the details.
A popular coin among collectors due to it being the largest Danish 
circulation copper coin of the 19th century (tied with the 2 Rigs -
bankskilling 1818); in addition, 1842 saw 13 types and sub-types 
struck making it a highly popular year to collect. 
€700-€900
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17179
DENMARK. Copper Pattern Rigsbankskilling, 1842. Co-
penhagen Mint. Christian VIII. NGC MS-64 Brown. KM-
Pn54; Hede-14; Sieg-unnumbered, page 291 (2018); Schou-12; 
Bruun-8146. Mintmaster: Georg Vilhelm Svendsen. Engraver: 
Frederik Christopher Krohn (FK). Superbly struck and present-
ing an excellent brown color with bursts of red.
This very rare pattern was rejected out of fear that a potential sil-
vering could make it pass as a 32 Rigsbankskilling (Sieg-5; KM-
734). 
€2,000-€2,500
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

========== PAGE 100 ==========

Page 84 Stack’s Bowers Galleries
The L.  E. Bruun Co llection • A Corpus of Scandinavian Monetary History Part IV
    
17180
DENMARK. 32 Rigsbankskilling, 1842. Altona Mint. Christian VIII. NGC MS-64+ Prooflike. KM-734; Hede-5A; Sieg-11; Schou-2; 
Bruun-8150. Mintmaster: Johan Friedrich Freund (FF). Engraver: Frederik Christopher Krohn (FK). NGC TOP POP . A magnificently 
toned proof-like stunner bound to attract the attention of even the most discriminative collectors.
The dies for the minting were engraved in Copenhagen. To accommodate requisitions from the Duchies of Schleswig and Holstein the re-
verse also carried the denomination, 10 Schill(ing) Courant. 
€900-€1,200
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
  
Photo Enlarged 2x
17181
DENMARK. 4 Rigsbankskilling, 1842. Altona Mint. Christian VIII. NGC MS-66. KM-721.3; Hede-8C; Sieg-8.3; Schou-6; Bru -
un-8152. Mintmaster: Johan Friedrich Freund (FF). Engraver: Frederik Christopher Krohn (FK). NGC TOP POP . Extremely rare 
variation struck in Altona in the Duchies. 
While more than one million were minted of this type in 1842, only a handful are known to have survived. The majority were not put into 
circulation but kept as a reserve in Altona. When the city fell to the rebels in 1848, the coins were seized and melted down. 
€3,000-€5,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

========== PAGE 101 ==========

 Bid Online at www.StacksBowers.com P age 85
Session I • Tuesday , March 24, 2026 • 12:00  PM ET / 5:00  PM CET
    
17182
DENMARK. Uniface Bronze Trial Strike Christian d'Or, 1843. Copenhagen Mint. Christian VIII. NGC MS-64. Fr-cf. 290; KM-cf. 
730; Hede-cf. 2; Sieg-cf. 15; Schou-1a; Bruun-8158. Weight: 12.83 gms. Mintmaster: Georg Vilhelm Svendsen (VS). Engraver: Frederik 
Christopher Krohn. Near Gem with an attractive color and razor sharp details, including the small crown mintmark (for Copenhagen) 
and the mintmaster’s initials.
A unique and highly interesting Trial-strike of the utmost numismatic significance. In all the reference works only the Altona type is men-
tioned, but - although the pattern was never realized - the present piece, which stems from the estate auction of the engraver, proves that 
there was indeed considerations of minting this gold trade coin in Copenhagen as well. 
€3,000-€4,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: H. H. J. Lynge Collection (Copenhagen - 1899) Lot # 2711.
Ex: F. C. Krohn (Copenhagen - 1884) Lot # 646.
Superlative Prooflike Gem and Excessively Rare
17183
DENMARK. Speciedaler, 1843. Copenhagen Mint. Christian VIII. NGC MS-65+ Prooflike. Dav-74; KM-720.1; 
Hede-3D; Sieg-13.4; Schou-2, Bruun-8159. Weight: 28.85 gms. Mintmaster: Georg Vilhelm Svendsen (VS). Engraver: 
Harald Conradsen (H. CONRADSEN). With only about a handful in private ownership, this beautiful Speciedaler, 
which should probably be seen as a presentation piece, will always be in high demand. A superlative Prooflike Gem with 
a delicious toning like the present can however best be described as a chance in a lifetime.
€8,000-€12,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

17190
DENMARK. 2 Christian d’Or, 1847. Altona Mint. Christian 
VIII. NGC MS-65. Fr-289; KM-722.2; Hede-1B; Sieg-16.2; 
Schou-1; Bruun-8184. Weight: 13.29 gms. Mintmaster: Johan 
Friedrich Freund (FF). Engraver: Christen Christensen (CC). 
NGC TOP POP . A beautiful Gem Mint State with exceptional 
matte details in the imposing arms. 
€5,000-€6,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17191
DENMARK. Rigsbankdaler, 1847. Altona Mint. Christian 
VIII. NGC MS-64+. KM-735.2; Hede-4B; Sieg-12.2; Schou-6; 
Bruun-8187. Weight: 14.42 gms. Mintmaster: Johan Friedrich 
Freund (FF). Engraver: Frederik Christopher Krohn (FK). NGC

From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

17194
DENMARK. Speciedaler, 1848. Copenhagen Mint. Frederik 
VII. NGC MS-67 Prooflike. Dav-75; KM-742; Hede-3; Sieg-7; 
Schou-1; Bruun-8193. Weight: 28.94 gms. Mintmaster: Georg 
Vilhelm Svendsen (VS). Engraver: Frederik Christopher Krohn 
(FK). NGC TOP POP: The best possibly obtainable example of 
this extremely popular accession type. On the obverse, the proof-
like surface are enveloped by in a subtle, multicolor toning which 
still allows for the luster to shine; the reverse has developed a 
darker multicolor which in the most perfect way emphasizes 
both the luster and the artistry of the design.

From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

NGC TOP POP . A most astonishing example of this lowly cop-
per, single-year Skilling, ablaze with luster and color.
This coin must be considered a presentation piece, most likely pre-
sented to the king himself for approval.

Superbly Toned Prooflike and TOP POP for Type
17199
DENMARK. Speciedaler, 1853. Copenhagen Mint. Frederik VII. NGC MS-67 Prooflike. Dav-76; KM-744.1; Hede-
4A; Sieg-6.1; Schou-3; Bruun-8213. Weight: 28.93 gms. Mintmaster: Georg Vilhelm Svendsen (VS). Engraver: Frederik 
Christopher Krohn (FK). NGC TOP POP for date and type. Superbly toned and designated MS67 Prooflike for good 
reason, this awe-inspiring Speciedaler would be the jewel in the crown in even the best of collections of 19th century 
Danish silver.
€8,000-€10,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17200
DENMARK. Rigsbankskilling, 1853. Copenhagen Mint. Frederik VII. NGC PROOF-65 Brown. KM-756; Hede-12C; Sieg-4; 
Schou-5; Bruun-8215. Mintmaster: Georg Vilhelm Svendsen (VS). Engraver: Frederik Christopher Krohn (FK). NGC TOP POP . Most 
likely a presentation piece, this proof coin shows the most delightful surfaces with red luster playing under a soft touch of multicolor 
toning. 
€600-€700
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

========== PAGE 106 ==========

Page 90 Stack’s Bowers Galleries
The L.  E. Bruun Co llection • A Corpus of Scandinavian Monetary History Part IV
    
17201
DENMARK. 2 Frederik d’Or, 1853. Altona Mint. Frederik VII. NGC MS-64. KM-750.2; Hede-1B; Sieg-16.2; Schou-1; Bruun-8216. 
Weight: 13.27 gms. Mintmaster: Johan Friedrich Freund (FF). Engraver: Frederik Christopher Krohn (FK). NGC TOP POP . Rare, one-
year type with silky surfaces, a few small bagmarks.
The Christian and Frederik d’Or coinage The first minting of d’Or coinage in Denmark took place in 1771 and 1775 but it is believed to be 
a coinage for a single specific use the Duchies. 1826 saw a few Double d’Ors minted, but from 1827 minting took place on a larger scale. The 
coins, imitating the French Louis d’Ors, were issued solely for international trade, mostly with Hamburg merchants, and were not part of 
the Danish coin circulation. Except for the 1802 Ducat, d’Or coins were the only Danish gold coins of the 19th century up until the intro-
duction of the decimal coinage in 1873. 
€5,000-€6,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Uncontested Superb Gem One-Y ear Type
17202
DENMARK. Frederik d’Or, 1853. Altona Mint. Frederik VII. NGC MS-67. Fr-292; KM-757; Hede-2; Sieg-15; 
Schou-2; Bruun-8217. Weight: 6.64 gms. Mintmaster: Johan Friedrich Freund (FF). Engraver: Frederik Christopher 
Krohn (FK). NGC TOP POP . A captivating Superb-Gem piece that should generate fierce competition.
As opposed to the Double d’ Ors that were struck in both Copenhagen and Altona for several dates between 1850 and 1863, 
the very rare 1 Frederik d’ Or was only struck in Altona in 1853.
€12,000-€16,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

========== PAGE 107 ==========

 Bid Online at www.StacksBowers.com P age 91
Session I • Tuesday , March 24, 2026 • 12:00  PM ET / 5:00  PM CET
    
17203
DENMARK. 2 Rigsdaler, 1854. Copenhagen Mint. Frederik 
VII. NGC MS-66 Prooflike. Dav-77; KM-761.2; Hede-6A; Sieg-
14.1; Schou-3; Bruun-8220. Mintmaster: Georg Vilhelm Svend-

TOP POP . A fabulous near-Superb Gem piece which must have 
served as some kind of presentation piece. The prodigious proof-
like quality of the king’s portrait truly showcases the artistic skill 
of Krohn. 
€5,000-€7,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

(FK). NGC TOP POP . Soft multicolor toning adorns the proof-
like surfaces and plays with the luster with delightful eye-appeal. 
This type was struck in both Altona in the duchies (mintmark orb)

From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

TOP POP . Dark, multicolor and golden toning is spreading from

trous, Proof-like coin.

DENMARK. Silver Pattern 16 Skilling Rigsmønt, 1854. Co-

Rare and delightfully toned Premium Gem uncirculated pattern 
and the only example graded by NGC. A regular issue with the 
same design was minted and put into circulation in 1856.

Session I • Tuesday , March 24, 2026 • 12:00  PM ET / 5:00  PM CET
Beauty Incarnate and Transcending Comparison
17211
DENMARK. 2 Rigsdaler, 1855. Copenhagen Mint. Frederik VII. NGC MS-69. Dav-77; KM-761.2; Hede-6A; Sieg-
14.1; Schou-2; Bruun-8229. Mintmaster: Georg Vilhelm Svendsen (VS). Engraver: Frederik Christopher Krohn (FK). A 
mere one point short of absolute perfection it hardly comes as a surprise that this piece is TOP POP by far, but actually 
the eye appeal totally transcends comparison. Everything from the sublime, iridescent toning to the stupendous luster 
and the perfect, matte devices catapults this piece into being what could arguably be said to be the most attractive of all 
Danish large silver coins from the 19th century and could be the crowning achievement for any collector of “modern” 
Danish coinage.
€15,000-€20,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17212
DENMARK. Rigsdaler, 1855. Copenhagen Mint. Frederik VII. NGC MS-67 Prooflike. KM-760.1; Hede-8A; Sieg-13.1; Schou-4; 
Bruun-8230. Weight: 14.42 gms. Mintmaster: Georg Vilhelm Svendsen (VS). Engraver: Frederik Christopher Krohn (FK). NGC TOP 
POP . Light golden hues on astonishing, mirror-like surfaces. 
€1,500-€2,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

17218
DENMARK. Copper Piefort 4 Skilling Rigsmønt, 1856. Co-
penhagen Mint. Frederik VII. NGC MS-65 Brown. KM-cf. 
758.2; Hede-cf. 11B; Sieg-cf. 10.2; Schou-unlisted; JS-p. 247; Bru-
un-8240. Mintmaster: Georg Vilhelm Svendsen (VS). Engraver: 
Frederik Christopher Krohn (FK). A superbly struck Gem with 
an exquisite multicolored copper toning.
Although an example was offered in the sale of A. Benzon in 1888 
(lot 3279), this excessively rare and highly unusual piefort surpris-
ingly eluded the normally extremely thorough H. H. Schou, result-
ing in almost all subsequent numismatist having missed it as well. 
€2,000-€2,500
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17219
DENMARK. 2 Rigsdaler, 1856. Altona Mint. Frederik VII. 
NGC MS-66. Dav-77; KM-761.1; Hede-6B; Sieg-14.2; Schou-2; 
Bruun-8249. Weight: 28.78 gms. Mintmaster: Johan Friedrich

17220
DENMARK. 16 Skilling Rigsmønt, 1857. Copenhagen Mint. 
Frederik VII. NGC MS-67. KM-765; Hede-10B; Sieg-11; 
Schou-4; Bruun-8251. Mintmaster: Georg Vilhelm Svendsen 
(VS). Engraver: Frederik Christopher Krohn (FK). NGC TOP 
POP . Silky, frosty surfaces on this three-year type. 
€600-€700
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17221
DENMARK. Silver Pattern 2 Rigsdaler, 1857. Private Mint. 
Frederik VII. NGC MS-65. KM-PnA60; Hede-7; Sieg-20; 
Schou-2; Bruun-8254. Weight: 28.81 gms. Engraver: Peter Pe -
tersen (PP). Exceptionally lustrous Gem with a fascinating, 
matte portrait which is arguably among the very best of the king.
Whereas restrikes in silver of the variant with a straight foot on 2 
occur, the lightly reeded edge on the present - and much rarer - 
variant proves that it is indeed a contemporary pattern.
This extremely rare pattern was created by Peter Petersen (1810-
1892), and is particularly interesting in that it for the first time 
bears the king’s title in Danish and not in Latin as had hitherto 
been the norm. 
€5,000-€7,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

Page 96 Stack’s Bowers Galleries
The L.  E. Bruun Co llection • A Corpus of Scandinavian Monetary History Part IV
    
17222
DENMARK. 2 Rigsdaler, 1863. Copenhagen Mint. Frederik VII. NGC MS-67. Dav-77; KM-761.3; Hede-6C; Sieg-14.3; Schou-2; 
Bruun-8270. Weight: 28.84 gms. Mintmaster: Peter Rasmus Hinnerup (RH). Engraver: Frederik Christopher Krohn (FK). NGC TOP 
POP with prooflike surface and rich golden toning. 
The scarce 1863 date is the only one in the series to be struck by the mintmaster Peter Rasmus Hinnerup. 
€5,000-€7,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17223
DENMARK. 2 Rigsdaler, 1863. Copenhagen Mint. Christian IX. NGC PROOF-66. Dav-78; KM-770; Hede-3; Sieg-4; Schou-1; 
Bruun-8280. Weight: 28.90 gms. Mintmaster: Peter Rasmus Hinnerup (RH). Engravers: Harald Conradsen (HC), Frederik Krohn 
(FK). Whereas condition-wise this almost superb Proof-66 could be confused with the existing restrikes, the reeded edge proves that 
this is an official, contemporary strike of unsurpassed beauty. 
Struck in commemoration of the accession of Christian IX of Schleswig-Holstein-Sonderburg-Glückstadt following the death of Frederik 
VII on 15 November 1863 and thereby ending more than 400 years of kings from the House of Oldenburg. 
€3,000-€5,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17224
DENMARK. 2 Rigsdaler, 1864. Copenhagen Mint. Christian IX. NGC MS-66+. Dav-79; KM-772.1; Hede-4A; Sieg-5.1; Schou-1; 
Bruun-8281. Weight: 28.88 gms. Mintmaster: Peter Rasmus Hinnerup (RH). Engraver: Harald Conradsen (HC). NGC TOP POP . A 
near Superb-Gem with an enchanting lambent patina filled with blue and purple tones. 
€5,000-€7,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

Unbelievably Attractive TOP POP Double D’Or
17225
DENMARK. 2 Christian d’Or, 1866. Copenhagen Mint. Christian IX. NGC MS-68. Fr-293; KM-773.1; Hede-1A; 
Sieg-7.1; Schou-1; Bruun-8282. Weight: 13.27 gms. Mintmaster: Peter Rasmus Hinnerup (RH). Engraver: Harald Con-
radsen (HC). NGC TOP POP . Appealingly, lightly toned and unbelievable attractive.
The Christian and Frederik d’Or coinage The first minting of d’Or coinage in Denmark took place in 1771 and 1775 but it 
is believed to be a coinage for a single specific use the Duchies. 1826 saw a few Double d’Ors minted, but from 1827 minting 
took place on a larger scale. The coins, imitating the French Louis d’Ors, were issued solely for international trade, mostly 
with Hamburg merchants, and were not part of the Danish coin circulation. Except for the 1802 Ducat, d’Or coins were the 
only Danish gold coins of the 19th century up until the introduction of the decimal coinage in 1873.
€18,000-€22,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17226
DENMARK. 2 Christian d’Or, 1869. Copenhagen Mint. Christian IX. NGC MS-64 H. Fr-293; KM-773.2; Hede-1B; Sieg-7.2; 
Schou-1; Bruun-8290. Weight: 13.28 gms. Mintmaster: Diderik Christian Andreas Svendsen (CS). Engraver: Harald Conradsen (HC). 
NGC TOP POP . A couple of coppery spots on otherwise fully lustrous uncirculated surfaces don’t detract from the overall charisma 
of this penultimate Danish gold coin before the Coinage Act of 1873. 
€10,000-€12,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

========== PAGE 114 ==========

Page 98 Stack’s Bowers Galleries
The L.  E. Bruun Co llection • A Corpus of Scandinavian Monetary History Part IV
One of the Most Desirable of All Danish 19th Century Gold Coins
17227
DENMARK. 2 Christian d’Or, 1870. Copenhagen Mint. Christian IX. NGC MS-69. Fr-293; KM-773.2; Hede-1B; 
Sieg-7.2; Schou-1; Bruun-8294. Weight: 13.29 gms. Mintmaster: Diderik Christian Andreas Svendsen (CS). Engraver: 
Harald Conradsen (HC). NGC TOP POP for date, type and denomination. In its league of its own, towering above all 
other examples, this piece of almost pure perfection, is quite simply one of the most desirable of all known Danish gold 
coins from the 19th century and bound to attract a wide international interest from the most discerning collectors.
This extremely rare issue with only a very few in private ownership is the last Danish gold coin minted before the introduc-
tion of the decimal system in 1873.
€30,000-€40,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17228
DENMARK. Skilling Rigsmønt, 1870. Copenhagen Mint. Christian IX. NGC MS-67 Red Brown. KM-774; Hede-6; Sieg-2; 
Schou-3; Bruun-8296. Mintmaster: Diderik Christian Andreas Svendsen (CS). Engraver: Frederik Christopher Krohn. NGC TOP 
POP for date, tied with two for type. Bright lustrous example of this small copper denomination, rarely encountered in high MS 
grades. Nearest rival for date in the NGC census is an MS-64.
The last 1 Skilling type to be minted before the introduction of the decimal coinage in 1873. 
€600-€700
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
