17232
DENMARK. 10 Kroner, 1873. Copenhagen Mint. Chris -
tian IX. NGC MS-67. Fr-296; KM-790.1; Hede-9A; Sieg-1.1; 
Schou-3; Bruun-8305. Weight: 4.48 gms. Mintmaster: Diderik 
Christian Andreas Svendsen (CS). Engraver: Harald Conradsen 
(HC). NGC TOP POP . Mirror-like surfaces on this the first year 
of emission and indeed the first year of Kroner and Øre, which 
are still the currency of Denmark. 
€3,600-€4,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17233
DENMARK. 20 Kroner, 1874. Copenhagen Mint. Chris -
tian IX. NGC MS-66+. Fr-295; KM-791.1; Hede-8A; Sieg-1.1; 
Schou-2; Bruun-8307. Weight: 8.96 gms. Mintmaster: Diderik 
Christian Andreas Svendsen (CS). Engraver: Harald Conradsen 
(HC). NGC TOP POP by a large margin with its nearest compet-
itor an MS-63. This key date, by far the most sought of the series, 
has eye-appeal which goes even beyond its impressive grade. 
€10,000-€12,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

========== PAGE 116 ==========

Page 100 Stack’s Bowers Galleries
The L.  E. Bruun Co llection • A Corpus of Scandinavian Monetary History Part IV
    
  
Photo Enlarged 2x
17234
DENMARK. 10 Kroner, 1874. Copenhagen Mint. Chris -
tian IX. NGC MS-67+. Fr-296; KM-790.1; Hede-9A; Sieg-1.1; 
Schou-3; Bruun-8308. Weight: 4.48 gms. Mintmaster: Diderik 
Christian Andreas Svendsen (CS). Engraver: Harald Conradsen 
(HC). NGC TOP POP . A lustrous example of this popular type 
with the sheaf and dolphin representing agriculture and fishing, 
the two corner stone of Danish economy at the time. 
€4,000-€5,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
  
Photo Enlarged 2x
17235
DENMARK. 10 Øre, 1874. Copenhagen Mint. Christian 
IX. NGC MS-67+. KM-795.1; Hede-16A; Sieg-9.1; Schou-5; 
Bruun-8310. Mintmaster: Diderik Christian Andreas Svendsen 
(CS). Engraver: Harald Conradsen. NGC TOP POP . Enchanting 
example of the smallest silver denomination of the time and the 
very first year of issue of the Øre denominations which are still 
used today. 
€800-€900
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17236
DENMARK. 5 Øre, 1874. Copenhagen Mint. Christian IX. 
NGC MS-66 Red. KM-794.1; Hede-17A; Sieg-1.1; Schou-6; 
Bruun-8311. Mintmaster: Diderik Christian Andreas Svendsen 
(CS). Engraver: Harald Conradsen. NGC TOP POP . Lustrous, 
red surfaces on this large, copper, “dolphin” type 5 Øre coin; a 
type prone to blemishing which ads to the magnificence of this 
example.
All denominations of Kroner and Øre coins of Christian IX had a 
dolphin (porpoise) and a sheaf of wheat on the reverse, signifying 
the importance of fishery and agriculture. The “dolphin” type coins 
are the also the first of the decimal coinage and highly popular. 
€600-€800
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17237
DENMARK. 2 Kroner, 1875. Copenhagen Mint. Christian 
IX. NGC MS-66. KM-798.1; Hede-13A; Sieg-1.1; Schou-1; Bru-
un-8317. Weight: 14.99 gms. Mintmaster: Diderik Christian An-
dreas Svendsen (CS). Engraver: Harald Conradsen (HC). NGC 
TOP POP . Handsome, dark toning has started to spread from 
the edges.
The silver 2 Kroner coins circulated from 1875 till 1923 and only 
six dates were struck with 1875 being the first. Because of the lon-
gevity of the usage and the delicacy of silver, the 1875 1 and 2 Kro-
ner are almost always found very worn - making the condition of 
this coin even more amazing. 
€1,000-€1,200
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

========== PAGE 117 ==========

 Bid Online at www.StacksBowers.com P age 101
Session I • Tuesday , March 24, 2026 • 12:00  PM ET / 5:00  PM CET
    
17238
DENMARK. Krone, 1875. Copenhagen Mint. Christian IX. 
NGC PROOF-68. KM-797.1; Hede-14A; Sieg-1.1; Schou-2; 
Bruun-8318. Weight: 7.52 gms. Mintmaster: Diderik Christian 
Andreas Svendsen (CS). Engraver: Harald Conradsen (HC). 
NGC TOP POP . Brown and multicolor toning, slightly patchy 
and adding to the allure of this spectacular survivor. 
€2,000-€2,500
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17239
DENMARK. Silver Pattern Krone, 1875. Copenhagen Mint. 
Christian IX. NGC PROOF-67+. KM-PnA63; cf. Hede-14A; 
Sieg-1; Schou-3; Bruun-8319. Weight: 7.47 gms. Mintmaster: 
Diderik Christian Andreas Svendsen (CS). Engraver: Harald 
Conradsen. A staggering superbly toned Gem with an entranc -
ing eye appeal throughout. Although these very rare patterns, 
without the engraver’s initials HC on the truncation of the neck, 
when found tend to be in nice condition, this stunner of a coin 
is absolutely peerless. 
€3,500-€4,500
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17240

tian IX. NGC MS-67+. Fr-295; KM-791.1; Hede-8A; Sieg-1.1; 
Schou-1; Bruun-8324. Weight: 8.96 gms. Mintmaster: Diderik 
Christian Andreas Svendsen (CS). Engraver: Harald Conradsen 
(HC). NGC TOP POP . Light, dusty toning covers both sides for 
increased eye-appeal. 
€4,500-€6,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17241

IX. NGC MS-67+ Prooflike. KM-798.1; Hede-13A; Sieg-1.1;

Christian Andreas Svendsen (CS). Engraver: Harald Conrad -
sen (HC). NGC TOP POP; Dark and golden multicolor toning 
spreading on the obverse, same but weaker on the reverse.  
€2,000-€2,500
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

DENMARK. Krone, 1876. Copenhagen Mint. Christian IX. 
NGC MS-67. KM-797.1; Hede-14A; Sieg-1.1; Schou-3; Bru -
un-8326. Weight: 7.59 gms. Mintmaster: Diderik Christian An-
dreas Svendsen (CS). Engraver: Harald Conradsen (HC). NGC 
TOP POP . Delightful, millefleur toning.

17243
DENMARK. 20 Kroner, 1877. Copenhagen Mint. Chris -
tian IX. NGC MS-68. Fr-295; KM-791.1; Hede-8A; Sieg-1.1; 
Schou-1; Bruun-8329. Weight: 8.94 gms. Mintmaster: Diderik 
Christian Andreas Svendsen (CS). Engraver: Harald Conradsen 
(HC). NGC TOP POP . Faint toning over fully lustrous surfaces.
20 Kroner is still the largest circulation issue coin of Denmark.

17248
DENMARK. 2 Øre, 1887. Copenhagen Mint. Christian IX. 
NGC MS-67 Brown. KM-793.1; Hede-18A; Sieg-1.1; Schou-1; 
Bruun-8357. Mintmaster: Diderik Christian Andreas Svendsen 
(CS). Engraver: Harald Conradsen. NGC TOP POP and a key 
date for the series. Very pleasing surfaces on this small copper 
denomination.
All denominations of Kroner and Øre coins of Christian IX had a 
dolphin (porpoise) and a sheaf of wheat on the reverse, signifying 
the importance of fishery and agriculture. The “dolphin” type coins 
are the also the first of the decimal coinage and highly popular. 
€1,000-€1,200
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17249
DENMARK. Commemorative 2 Kroner, 1888. Copenhagen 
Mint. Christian IX. NGC MS-68. KM-799; Hede-10; Sieg-1; 
Schou-1; Bruun-8359. Weight: 14.98 gms. Mintmaster: Diderik 
Christian Andreas Svendsen (CS). Engraver: Harald Conradsen 
(HC). NGC TOP POP . Bright, silvery surfaces and razor-sharp 
features with a hint of gray patina.
Struck on the 25th anniversary of the king’s accession. Christian IX 
(1863-1906) was the first Danish king from the house of Glücks -
borg.  
€700-€1,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

17265
DENMARK. 2 Kroner, 1903. Copenhagen Mint. Christian IX. 
NGC MS-68. KM-802; Hede-12; Sieg-3; Schou-1; Bruun-8400. 
Weight: 14.98 gms. Mintmaster: Vilhelm Burchard Poulsen (P). 
Engraver: Gunnar Jensen (GJ). NGC TOP POP; A coin of ex-
traordinary beauty; the design, the condition and the luster com-
bines to a thing of true beauty.
Struck to commemorate the 40th anniversary of King Christian 
IX’s reign, this was the first of many coins to be designed and en-
graved by Gunnar Jensen. 
€800-€1,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17266
DENMARK. Experimental Commemorative 2 Krone, 1903. 
Copenhagen Mint. Christian IX. NGC MS-66 Matte. KM-
802; Hede-12; Sieg-3; Schou-1; Bruun-8401. Weight: 14.97 gms. 
Mintmaster: Vilhelm Burchard Poulsen (P). Engraver: Knud 
Gunnar Jensen (GJ). A flawless and exceedingly charming Gem. 
A highly interesting and exceptionally rare matte pattern experi-
menting with the French medal technique of the time. Similar 
matte strikes are known for the medallic issues without denomi-
nation (KM-X1). 
€2,000-€3,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

X. NGC MS-68+. Fr-300; KM-816; Hede-2; Sieg-3; Schou-2; 
Bruun-8449. Weight: 4.49 gms. Mintmaster: Vilhelm Burchard 
Poulsen (VBP). Engraver: Andreas Frederik Vilhelm Hansen 
(AH). NGC TOP POP for date and type. The first year of this 
two-year type; mirror-like in the fields and crisp details through-
out.
Interestingly this type, both the 10 and the 20 Kroner gold coins, 
have the dates on both sides.  
€4,000-€5,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17280
DENMARK. 5 Øre, 1913. Copenhagen Mint. Christian X. 
NGC MS-67 Brown. KM-814.1; Hede-14A; Sieg-3.1; Schou-4; 
Bruun-8451. Mintmaster: Vilhelm Burchard Poulsen (VBP). 
Engraver: Gunnar Jensen (GJ). NGC TOP POP . Pristine, blaz -
ing surfaces with blue tinting on this key date and the first in the 
series.
Surely, L. E. Bruun obtained the Danish coins from the latter days 
of his life directly from the Royal Mint.  
€1,500-€2,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17281
DENMARK. 2 Øre, 1913. Copenhagen Mint. Christian X. 
NGC MS-67 Brown. KM-813; Hede-16A; Sieg-3.1; Schou-5; 
Bruun-8452. Mintmaster: Vilhelm Burchard Poulsen (VBP). 
Engraver: Gunnar Jensen (GJ). NGC TOP POP . Rich, orange-
brown surfaces with flashes of blue throughout. 
€600-€700
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

========== PAGE 125 ==========

 Bid Online at www.StacksBowers.com P age 109
Session I • Tuesday , March 24, 2026 • 12:00  PM ET / 5:00  PM CET
    
17282
DENMARK. 20 Kroner, 1914. Copenhagen Mint. Christian X. 
NGC MS-68+. Fr-299; KM-817.1; Hede-1A; Sieg-3.1; Schou-1; 
Bruun-8455. Weight: 8.95 gms. Mintmaster: Vilhelm Burchard 
Poulsen (VBP). Engraver: Andreas Frederik Vilhelm Hansen 
(AH). NGC TOP POP for date and sub-type. Only very light 
toning on the rich, golden surfaces. 
€5,000-€6,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
  
Photo Enlarged 2x
17283
DENMARK. 25 Øre, 1914. Copenhagen Mint. Christian X. 
NGC MS-68+. KM-815.1; Hede-10A; Sieg-3.1; Schou-2; Bru -
un-8456. Mintmaster: Vilhelm Burchard Poulsen (VBP). En -
graver: Gunnar Jensen (GJ). NGC TOP POP , a key date and the 
rarest of the short series. orange-brown and multicolor toning 
is building from the peripheries for a charming contrast to the 
bright, uncirculated surfaces. 
€800-€1,200
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17284
DENMARK. 2 Kroner, 1915. Copenhagen Mint. Christian X. 
NGC MS-68+. KM-820; Hede-8; Sieg-2; Schou-2; Bruun-8461. 
Weight: 14.99 gms. Mintmaster: Vilhelm Burchard Poulsen 
(VBP). Engraver: Andreas Frederik Vilhelm Hansen (AH). NGC 
TOP POP - only surpassed for type by the 1916 which is also of-
fered in this sale. A darker multicolor toning hugs the devices on 
the obverse, less so on the reverse. 
The 2 Kroner in silver was introduced in 1875. Only six dates were 
struck before the design and metal was changed in 1924, with 1916 
being the last year of minting. 1915 and 1916 reuse the obverse 
of the 1912 commemorative and the reverse of the Christian IX 2 
Kroner.  
€700-€1,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17285
DENMARK. 20 Kroner, 1916. Copenhagen Mint. Christian X. 
NGC MS-67+. Fr-299; KM-817.1; Hede-1A; Sieg-3.1; Schou-1; 
Bruun-8467. Weight: 8.96 gms. Mintmaster: Vilhelm Burchard 
Poulsen (VBP). Engraver: Andreas Frederik Vilhelm Hansen 
(AH). NGC TOP POP . Light toning over handsome surfaces. 
€4,000-€5,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17286
DENMARK. 2 Kroner, 1916. Copenhagen Mint. Christian X. 
NGC MS-69. KM-820; Hede-8; Sieg-2; Schou-2; Bruun-8468. 
Weight: 15.01 gms. Mintmaster: Vilhelm Burchard Poulsen 
(VBP). Engraver: Andreas Frederik Vilhelm Hansen (AH). NGC 
TOP POP both for date and type. Bright, beautiful surfaces with 
traces of multicolor toning.
€1,200-€1,800
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17287
DENMARK. 5 Øre, 1916. Copenhagen Mint. Christian X. 
NGC MS-68 Brown. KM-814.1; Hede-14A; Sieg-3.1; Schou-6; 
Bruun-8472. Mintmaster: Vilhelm Burchard Poulsen (VBP). En-
graver: Gunnar Jensen (GJ).
NGC TOP POP for both date and type. Impossibly as-struck and 
surely hand-picked from the Royal Mint - a stunning example of 
the largest copper coin of the time. 
€800-€1,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

========== PAGE 126 ==========

Page 110 Stack’s Bowers Galleries
The L.  E. Bruun Co llection • A Corpus of Scandinavian Monetary History Part IV
    
  
Photo Enlarged 2x
17288
DENMARK. 10 Kroner, 1917. Copenhagen Mint. Christian 
X. NGC MS-68. Fr-300; KM-816; Hede-2; Sieg-3; Schou-2; 
Bruun-8476. Weight: 4.48 gms. Mintmaster: Vilhelm Burchard 
Poulsen (VBP). Engraver: Andreas Frederik Vilhelm Hansen 
(AH). NGC TOP POP - only surpassed for type by the MS-68+ 
1913 also offered in this sale. Boldly lustrous.  
€3,000-€4,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
  
Photo Enlarged 2x
17289
DENMARK. 25 Øre, 1918. Copenhagen Mint. Christian X. 
NGC MS-69. KM-815.1; Hede-10A; Sieg-3.1; Schou-1; Bru -
un-8482. Mintmaster: Vilhelm Burchard Poulsen (VBP). En -
graver: Gunnar Jensen (GJ). NGC TOP POP for both date and 
type. The penultimate date for silver currency coinage in Den -
mark. The nottled, brown patina on the obverse cannot subdue 
the bright, silvery surfaces on this coin. 
€800-€1,200
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
17290
DENMARK. 5 Øre, 1919. Copenhagen Mint. Christian X. 
NGC MS-67. KM-814.2a; Hede-15B; Sieg-3.3; Schou-4; Bru-
un-8490. Mintmaster: Hans Christian Nielsen (HCN). Engraver: 
Gunnar Jensen (GJ). NGC TOP POP for both type and date. 
Single-year subtype in zinc-clad iron with mintmaster’s initials 
HCN. A type easily susceptible to rust with this example in pris-
tine condition, as struck, and scarce so.
In 1918, as the price of copper had soared during World War One, 
it was decided to strike Øre coins in iron covered by a thin layer 
of Zinc, giving them the characteristic oxidized, black surfaces. 
Only two dates were struck, 1918 and 1919, and it turned out to 
be a costly affair; the dies kept breaking and on recalling them the 
metal had no value and couldn’t be re-used. The recalled coins were 
therefore poured into the concrete foundations of the new Mint on 
Amager Boulevard in Copenhagen, which was being built at the 
time. 
€1,000-€1,500
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
  
Photo Enlarged 2x
17291
DENMARK. 10 Øre, 1923. Copenhagen Mint. Christian X. 
NGC MS-68. KM-818.2a; Hede-13; Sieg-11.3; Schou-2; Bru -
un-8509. Mintmaster: Hans Christian Nielsen (HCN). Engraver: 
Gunnar Jensen (GJ). NGC TOP POP - tied for type. Lustrous, 
pristine surface on this key date, which is one of the most cov -
eted Danish decimal coins.
Not many 10 Øre coins were struck in 1923 as a new series was 
underway. The same goes for the 5 øre 1923, while no 25 Øre was 
minted that year. 
€2,000-€3,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
End of Session I
